behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (catalog availability filter + ranker merge), not just one function. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): a unit test for `is_in_stock` can pass while integration still leaks out-of-stock SKUs when the ranking stage re-joins full catalog by score. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/LLM-output dependence, no multi-agent chain, so not System/Agentic/Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires recommendation pipeline filtering/ranking, not real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender with in-process catalog repo + ranking module wired together; seed products where top relevance items are out-of-stock; assert final ranked page contains zero out-of-stock IDs.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on wiring user-settings retrieval into filtering before/within ranking. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit tests for category filter logic can pass while blocked categories leak if settings mapper sends wrong category IDs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no model-output quality dependency; no multi-step agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is deterministic rule-based exclusion from explicit settings.
behaviors.test_description: Wire settings service/repo + filter + ranker with fixture user blocking categories; assert blocked categories are absent from output across pages.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when browsing-feature extraction and scoring are combined, not only inside a pure scoring function. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test of score formula may pass, but integration can fail if browsing events are windowed incorrectly before scoring, causing no uplift. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; behavior does not require nondeterministic AI/LLM judgment; single recommendation step only. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text describes deterministic ranking signals, not model-output quality from a real LLM/ML service.
behaviors.test_description: Use integrated pipeline with fixture browsing history and candidate set; assert items with recent-browse signal receive higher ranks than otherwise comparable items without that signal.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stability depends on interaction of sorting/tie-breaker + pagination modules. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test for paginator slice math can pass while integrated ordering is unstable due missing secondary sort key before pagination. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no external OS/network/subprocess needed; deterministic business logic; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about internal ordering/pagination with unchanged data, not real external infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Seed deterministic fixture with tie-score products; run full ranking+sort+paginate twice for same user state; assert page 2 item IDs and order are identical and page boundaries are correct.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this rule is enforced across ranking + diversification stage, not a single isolated function in many implementations. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test for diversification helper can pass while pipeline ordering applies diversification before hard filters, removing the serendipitous item later. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no real LLM/AI output dependence implied; single pipeline pass. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text specifies an explicit deterministic product-mix constraint, not subjective generation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run integrated recommendation flow with user history concentrated in one category; assert final top-N contains at least one item from an unseen category while still respecting stock/preference constraints.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: cold-start fallback requires interaction between history lookup and popularity strategy selection. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test of popularity ranker passes, but integrated flow can return empty if 'no-history' detection fails and never invokes fallback. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no LLM/AI-output quality dependence; single request flow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is fallback orchestration internal to service.
behaviors.test_description: Wire user-profile/history module with empty history fixture plus popularity source; request recommendations for new user and assert non-empty list ordered by popularity baseline.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at handoff between recommendation response metadata and tracking persistence schema. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test for tracking payload builder can pass while integration stores wrong position because UI/list index mapping differs from persisted field. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: can be validated with local DB/clock injection; no required subprocess/network/OS primitive; no AI dependency; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infra beyond local module wiring/persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendation list, simulate click on known position, persist tracking event through repository, then assert stored product_id, rank_position, and timestamp are correct and queryable.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: latency target is sensitive to real runtime, process scheduling, memory, and I/O behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repos/caches/subcomponents can always pass and hide serialization/query/allocation overhead. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit microbench of scorer misses end-to-end latency bottlenecks in data retrieval/sorting. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Integration): in-process integration with fakes misses real process/OS effects and production-like data-access costs that breach 200ms. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is a wall-clock SLA under large load, which requires real infrastructure timing.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end recommendation service in a real process with 100k-product dataset and production-like config; issue repeated requests and assert p95 (or specified metric) under 200ms after warm-up.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module state propagation (purchase event -> user profile/features -> next ranking). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test for 'deprioritizePurchased' passes, but integration fails when purchase event ingestion updates wrong user feature store key. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text; no LLM/AI-output quality dependence; not multi-agentic workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because requirement is sequential internal state update across requests, testable with local modules/db.
behaviors.test_description: Issue recommendations, record purchase of one recommended SKU, trigger feature/state update, request recommendations again, and assert purchased SKU rank drops versus prior request.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: determinism depends on full pipeline consistency (feature fetch, sorting tie-breakers, pagination), not just one function. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit test for comparator passes, but integration can still be nondeterministic if upstream candidate ordering varies and no stable secondary key is applied globally. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no real LLM/AI nondeterminism implied; single-step request behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text scopes determinism to unchanged internal user state, which can be validated in-process with integrated modules.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user/profile/catalog snapshots, run recommendation request twice (or N times) in same test run; assert exact ranked ID sequence is identical each time.
